perturbation.original_text: 3. All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. The Recipient shall honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information. The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party; however, the Recipient shall have a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use the Confidential Information for internal purposes. The Recipient is not obligated to return or destroy copies of Confidential Information. The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will not suffer irreparable injury if its Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement, and the disclosing Party shall not be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach, nor shall the disclosing Party be entitled to an award of actual or exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: This change grants the Recipient a license to use the Confidential Information and removes the obligation to return or destroy copies. It also eliminates the disclosing party's right to injunctive relief or damages in case of a breach. This directly contradicts the final sentence of section 8, which states the agreement does not create an obligation for either party to disclose information to the other or enter into any other agreement. The license to use confidential information effectively creates a new obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the Parties and in no way creates an obligation for either Party to disclose information to the other Party or to enter into any other agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 3 grants the Recipient a non-exclusive license to use the Confidential Information and removes the obligation to return or destroy copies. This directly contradicts section 8, which states the agreement creates no obligation for either party to disclose information or enter into any other agreement. By granting a license, the changed text effectively creates a new obligation, directly conflicting with the original intent and wording of section 8.

perturbation.original_text: 4. The terms of this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either Party's right to develop independently or acquire products without use of the other Party's Confidential Information. The disclosing party acknowledges that the Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement will prohibit the Recipient from developing or having developed for it products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Confidential Information provided that the Recipient does not violate any of its obligations under this Agreement in connection with such development. CONFIDENTIAL Non-Disclosure Agreement
perturbation.changed_text: 4. The terms of this Agreement shall be construed to limit either Party's right to develop independently or acquire products without use of the other Party's Confidential Information. The disclosing party acknowledges that the Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement will prohibit the Recipient from developing or having developed for it products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Confidential Information provided that the Recipient does violate any of its obligations under this Agreement in connection with such development. CONFIDENTIAL Non-Disclosure Agreement
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall not' to 'shall' in the first sentence and 'does not violate' to 'does violate' in the final sentence of this section, the agreement now states the opposite intention. The agreement is intended as a Non-Disclosure Agreement. This change states the the agreement limits the receiving party's ability to develop similar products and that the recipient may violate their obligations under this agreement while doing so. This contradicts the entire purpose of the agreement as stated in the agreement's title and introductory paragraphs.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS Insight and the Second Party (the "Parties") have an interest in participating in discussions wherein either Party might share information with the other that the disclosing Party considers to be proprietary and confidential to itself ("Confidential Information"); and WHEREAS the Parties agree that Confidential Information of a Party might include, but not be limited to that Party's: (1) business plans, all contracts, methods, and practices; (2) personnel, customers, and suppliers; (3) inventions, processes, methods, products, patent applications, and other proprietary rights; or (4) specifications, drawings, sketches, models, samples, tools, computer programs, technical information, or other related information;
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent of the NDA, which is to protect confidential information. By changing 'shall not' to 'shall' and 'does not violate' to 'does violate', the agreement now states that it limits the recipient's ability to develop similar products and allows them to violate their obligations under the agreement. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which describes the purpose of the agreement as protecting confidential information and enabling discussions where such information might be shared. The change creates a strong and clear contradiction that undermines the entire purpose of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Notwithstanding the above, the Parties agree that information shall not be deemed Confidential Information and the Recipient shall have no obligation to hold in confidence such information, where such information: (a) Is already known to the Recipient, having been disclosed to the Recipient by a third party without such third party having an obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing Party; or (b) Is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Recipient, its employees, officers, directors, or agents; or (c) Is independently developed by the Recipient without reference to any Confidential Information disclosed hereunder; or (d) Is approved for release (and only to the extent so approved) by the disclosing Party; or (e) Is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement of a court or governmental agency or where required by operation of law.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Notwithstanding the above, the Parties agree that all information shall be deemed Confidential Information and the Recipient shall have an absolute obligation to hold in confidence such information, including information: (a) Already known to the Recipient, having been disclosed to the Recipient by a third party without such third party having an obligation of confidentiality to the disclosing Party; or (b) Is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of the Recipient, its employees, officers, directors, or agents; or (c) Is independently developed by the Recipient without reference to any Confidential Information disclosed hereunder; or (d) Is approved for release (and only to the extent so approved) by the disclosing Party; or (e) Is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement of a court or governmental agency or where required by operation of law.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word "not" from the first sentence of section 5, it creates a contradiction with the entire rest of the agreement, the agreement intends some information to be kept confidential. However, with this removal it is now stating all information is confidential, regardless of it's previous status.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS Insight and the Second Party (the "Parties") have an interest in participating in discussions wherein either Party might share information with the other that the disclosing Party considers to be proprietary and confidential to itself ("Confidential Information"); and WHEREAS the Parties agree that Confidential Information of a Party might include, but not be limited to that Party's: (1) business plans, all contracts, methods, and practices; (2) personnel, customers, and suppliers; (3) inventions, processes, methods, products, patent applications, and other proprietary rights; or (4) specifications, drawings, sketches, models, samples, tools, computer programs, technical information, or other related information;
perturbation.justification: The original text states conditions under which information is *not* deemed confidential. The changed text eliminates this possibility, stating that *all* information is deemed confidential and subject to an absolute obligation of confidence, 'including' information which was previously exempted. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which specifies that the agreement pertains to 'Confidential Information', implying that there is a subset of information that is confidential. If *all* information is confidential as the changed text states, then the contradicted text is nonsensical.

perturbation.original_text: 9. This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of two (2) years from the Effective Date unless otherwise terminated by either Party giving notice to the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement. The requirement to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of two (2) years from the Effective Date and may not be terminated by either Party giving notice to the other of its desire to terminate this Agreement. The requirement to protect Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall not survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change removes the option for either party to terminate the agreement early and states that the obligation to protect confidential information does not survive termination. This is a direct contradiction to section 3, which specifies what rights and remedies the disclosing party has. It is now a perpetual agreement, which contradicts the stated term of two years and the ability of either party to terminate the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. The Recipient shall honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information. The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 9 removes the ability for either party to terminate the agreement, making it a perpetual agreement for two years, and also states that confidentiality obligations do not survive termination. This directly contradicts Section 3, which grants the disclosing party specific rights and remedies regarding confidential information, including the right to seek injunctive relief and damages for breaches of confidentiality. The change makes it impossible to reconcile the two sections, creating a clear contradiction. If Section 9 states obligations don't survive termination, it contradicts section 3 which allows disclosing party to seek remedies for breaches, as remedies imply survival of obligations.
